details.text: Derivative: أَنذِرْ – This is the command (imperative) form of the verb أَنْذَرَ (to warn) which captures in this context of “We sent Nuh to his nation” that he (Nuh) was obligated or made to feel the obligation to warn his people. It has a stronger and more direct meaning than using the present tense of يُنْذِرُ.
Derivative: غَفَّارًا means to forgive over and over again, continuously or perpetually forgiving.
Derivative: مِّدْرَارًا is rain that comes in abundant supply.
Derivative: أَطْوَارًا is the plural of طَوْر (Tawr) which means a stage. أَطْوَارًا means “in stages” or “stage after stage”.
Derivative: فِجَاجًا is the plural of فَجٌّ which is a path between two mountains that facilitates travel between them.
Derivative: كَفَّارًا means someone who is extreme in disbelief or ingratitude i.e., in denial of the favours of Allah.

- He will forgive (يَغْفِرْ) for you (لَكُم) from (مِّن) your sins (ذُنُوبِكُمْ)”. Here the مِن (from) is for the purpose of تَبْعِيض (Tab’eed) which means to indicate to “some” of something. As such, مِّن ذُنُوبِكُمْ (from your sins) means “some of your sins”.

- The use of the Masdar اسْتِكْبَارًا following its verb اسْتَكْبَرُوا is known as the المَفْعُول المُطْلَق (Al-Maf’ul Al-Mutlaq) which is used to give greater emphasis to the verb, to mean “They were so arrogant” or “Extremely arrogant” or “In complete arrogance”.
